window_summary: This window tracks the recent rust-lang/rustlings release stream from version 3.0.0 through 6.5.0, capturing major rewrites, new exercises, CLI improvements, and breaking changes.

chunks.title: Rustlings 3.0.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: rust-lang/rustlings
Release: Rustlings 3.0.0
Tag: 3.0.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2020-04-11T22:04:24Z; created_at: 2020-04-11T22:01:08Z
Release notes:
See the [changelog](https://github.com/fmoko/rustlings/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md#300-2020-04-11) for what's changed.
As for what makes this a breaking release, in #278 we decided to make the `compile` exercise mode log its output to stdout. Since this changes existing user behaviour in a (broadly) backwards-incompatible way, we decided to make this a major release.
The good thing is that you don't need to do anything to upgrade! 🎉
To upgrade:
```sh
git fetch
git checkout tags/3.0.0
```
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the rust-lang/rustlings release 3.0.0, the compile exercise mode was changed to log its output to stdout, which was considered a breaking change.

chunks.title: Rustlings 4.0.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: rust-lang/rustlings
Release: Rustlings 4.0.0
Tag: 4.0.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2020-07-08T09:41:47Z; created_at: 2020-07-08T09:35:27Z
Release notes:
See the [changelog](https://github.com/rust-lang/rustlings/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#400-2020-07-08) for what's changed.
The important breaking changes that make this a major release are:
- `testX.rs` files have been renamed `quizX.rs`, due to people confusing them with the similarly-named `testsX.rs` exercises (which are about testing, not _being_ tested)
- You can now display the console output for tests using the new `--nocapture` option. This adds a new boolean argument to most CLI commands.
Thanks to all of the people who contributed! A full list can be seen [here](https://github.com/rust-lang/rustlings/compare/3.0.0...4.0.0), there's too many people for me to manually list here, sorry 😓
To upgrade:
```sh
git fetch
git checkout tags/4.0.0
```
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the rust-lang/rustlings release 4.0.0, testX.rs files were renamed to quizX.rs to avoid confusion with testsX.rs exercises, and a new --nocapture option was added to display console output for tests.

chunks.title: Rustlings 4.3.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: rust-lang/rustlings
Release: Rustlings 4.3.0
Tag: 4.3.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2020-12-29T10:41:19Z; created_at: 2020-12-29T10:39:26Z
Release notes:
See the [changelog](https://github.com/rust-lang/rustlings/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#4.3.0) for what's changed. Some highlights:
- The text you see when running `rustlings` (without any arguments) should now be more helpful
- The exercise order has been adjusted to more closely match the order of chapter in the Rust book
- A `rustlings list` command has been added
To upgrade:
```sh
git fetch
git checkout tags/4.3.0
```
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the rust-lang/rustlings release 4.3.0, the default help text was improved, the exercise order was adjusted to better match the Rust book, and a rustlings list command was added.

chunks.title: Rustlings 4.4.0
chunks.raw_text: Repository: rust-lang/rustlings Release: Rustlings 4.4.0 Tag: 4.4.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2021-04-24T10:04:01Z; created_at: 2021-04-24T09:57:00Z Release notes: See the [changelog](https://github.com/rust-lang/rustlings/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#4.4.0) for what's changed. Some highlights: - A _lot_ of small and large improvements to how exercises work - `rustlings list` now displays structured data, and a takes a lot more options to filter that data: - It also now shows you progress per-exercise, and a total summary of your completion: - The compliation times for Rustlings should be significantly sped up, we replaced the crate we use for CLI option parsing with something more lightweight - Rustlings now respects the `NO_EMOJI` envionment variable - A new exercise, `iterators5`, has been added To upgrade, run: ```sh git fetch git checkout tags/4.4.0 ```
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the rust-lang/rustlings release 4.4.0, many exercise improvements were made, rustlings list now displays structured data with more filtering options and per-exercise progress, compilation times were sped up, the NO_EMOJI environment variable is now respected, and a new iterators5 exercise was added.

chunks.title: Rustlings 4.6.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: rust-lang/rustlings
Release: Rustlings 4.6.0
Tag: 4.6.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2021-09-25T09:27:47Z; created_at: 2021-09-25T09:23:05Z
Release notes:
See the [changelog](https://github.com/rust-lang/rustlings/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#4.6.0) for what's changed. Some highlights:
- New exercises:
- `advanced_errs1` and `advanced_errs2`
- `modules3`
- `try_from_into` and `from_str` have been reworked to include custom error types.
- Watch mode now has a `quit` and `help` command.
- Watch mode now also shows you a nice farewell message when you exit it.
To upgrade, run:
```sh
git fetch
git checkout tags/4.6.0
```
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the rust-lang/rustlings release 4.6.0, new exercises advanced_errs1, advanced_errs2, and modules3 were added; try_from_into and from_str were reworked to include custom error types; watch mode gained quit and help commands and a farewell message.

chunks.title: Rustlings 4.7.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: rust-lang/rustlings
Release: Rustlings 4.7.0
Tag: 4.7.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2022-04-14T09:23:06Z; created_at: 2022-04-14T09:19:54Z
Release notes:
See the [changelog](https://github.com/rust-lang/rustlings/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#4.7.0) for what's changed. Some highlights:
- A new intro section prefixing the normal exercises has been added.
- New exercise, `move_semantics4`.
- `rust-analyzer` support has been added via an optional crate feature. More info in the [README](https://github.com/rust-lang/rustlings#enabling-rust-analyzer)
- Tons and tons of bug fixes.
To upgrade, run:
```sh
git fetch
git checkout tags/4.7.0
```
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the rust-lang/rustlings release 4.7.0, a new intro section was added, a new move_semantics4 exercise was added, rust-analyzer support was added via an optional crate feature, and many bug fixes were made.

chunks.title: Rustlings 5.0.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: rust-lang/rustlings
Release: Rustlings 5.0.0
Tag: 5.0.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2022-07-16T12:36:35Z; created_at: 2022-07-16T12:30:04Z
Release notes:
Probably Rustlings' biggest release yet! 🎉
For detailed information, check the [changelog](https://github.com/rust-lang/rustlings/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#500-2022-07-16). I'll post the features section here:
- Hint comments in exercises now also include a reference to the
`hint` watch mode subcommand.
- **intro1**: Added more hints to point the user to the source file.
- **variables**: Switched variables3 and variables4.
- Moved `vec` and `primitive_types` exercises before `move_semantics`.
- Renamed `vec` to `vecs` to be more in line with the naming in general.
- Split up the `collections` exercises in their own folders.
- **vec2**: Added a second part of the function that provides an alternative,
immutable way of modifying vec values.
- **enums3**: Added a hint.
- Moved `strings` before `modules`.
- Added a `strings3` exercise to teach modifying strings.
- Added a `hashmaps3` exercise for some advanced usage of hashmaps.
- Moved the original `quiz2` to be `strings4`, since it only tested strings
anyways.
- Reworked `quiz2` into a new exercise that tests more chapters.
- Renamed `option` to `options`.
- **options1**: Rewrote parts of the exercise to remove the weird array
iteration stuff.
- Moved `generics3` to be `quiz3`.
- Moved box/arc exercises behind `iterators`.
- **iterators4**: Added a test for factorials of zero.
- Split `threads1` between two exercises, the first one focusing more on
`JoinHandle`s.
- Added a `threads3` exercises that uses `std::sync::mpsc`.
- Added a `clippy3` exercises with some more interesting checks.
- **as_ref_mut**: Added a section that actually tests `AsMut`.
- Added 3 new lifetimes exercises.
- Added 3 new traits exercises.
### Summary
This release adds 12 new exercises, makes tons of little (and sometimes significant) improvements to existing exercises, rearranges a good chunk of the exercises to make more sense in our curriculum (while still trying to stay faithful to the book), and it gets rid of some exercises that have not been a good fit for this project.
There's still a fair amount of things that I want to change, such as having better exercises for smart pointers, but that's for a future release. Feel free to post your feedback after you've completed this new version of the course!
Special thanks for the Rust Foundation for sponsoring the majority of my work on this release!
To upgrade:
```sh
git fetch
git checkout tags/5.0.0
```
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the rust-lang/rustlings release 5.0.0, the biggest release yet, 12 new exercises were added, many existing exercises were improved and rearranged, some exercises were removed, and hint comments now include a reference to the hint watch mode subcommand.

chunks.title: Rustlings 6.0.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: rust-lang/rustlings
Release: Rustlings 6.0.0
Tag: v6.0.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2024-07-03T13:43:42Z; created_at: 2024-07-03T13:33:19Z
Release notes:
A rewrite with a ton of new features and changes ✨
See the [**CHANGELOG**](https://github.com/rust-lang/rustlings/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#600-2024-07-03) for the most important changes.
This version is not compatible with older versions. You need to start over with the exercises if you want to try it out. You could try copying some of your old solutions.
Follow the [instructions in the README](https://github.com/rust-lang/rustlings?tab=readme-ov-file#getting-started) for getting started 🚀
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the rust-lang/rustlings release v6.0.0, a rewrite was done with many new features and changes; it is not compatible with older versions and requires starting over with exercises.

chunks.title: Rustlings 6.2.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: rust-lang/rustlings
Release: Rustlings 6.2.0
Tag: v6.2.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2024-08-09T09:59:38Z; created_at: 2024-08-09T09:52:58Z
Release notes:
### Added
- Show a message before checking and running an exercise. This gives the user instant feedback and avoids confusion if the checks take too long.
- Show a helpful error message when trying to install Rustlings with a Rust version lower than the minimum one that Rustlings supports.
- Add a `README.md` file to the `solutions/` directory.
- Allow initializing Rustlings in a Cargo workspace.
- `dev check`: Check that all solutions are formatted with `rustfmt`.
### Changed
- Remove the state file and the solutions directory from the generated `.gitignore` file.
- Run the final check of all exercises in parallel.
- Small exercise improvements.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the rust-lang/rustlings release v6.2.0, a message is now shown before checking and running an exercise, a helpful error message is shown when installing with a too-low Rust version, a README.md was added to the solutions directory, initialization in a Cargo workspace is allowed, dev check now checks solution formatting, the state file and solutions directory were removed from the generated .gitignore, the final check of all exercises runs in parallel, and small exercise improvements were made.

chunks.title: Rustlings 6.3.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: rust-lang/rustlings
Release: Rustlings 6.3.0
Tag: v6.3.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2024-08-29T15:24:15Z; created_at: 2024-08-29T15:20:47Z
Release notes:
### Added
- Add the following exercise lints:
- `forbid(unsafe_code)`: You shouldn't write unsafe code in Rustlings.
- `forbid(unstable_features)`: You don't need unstable features in Rustlings and shouldn't rely on them while learning Rust.
- `forbid(todo)`: You forgot a `todo!()`.
- `forbid(empty_loop)`: This can only happen by mistake in Rustlings.
- `deny(infinite_loop)`: No infinite loops are needed in Rustlings.
- `deny(mem_forget)`: You shouldn't leak memory while still learning Rust.
- Show a link to every exercise file in the list.
- Add scroll padding in the list.
- Break the help footer of the list into two lines when the terminal width isn't big enough.
- Enable scrolling with the mouse in the list.
- `dev check`: Show the progress of checks.
- `dev check`: Check that the length of all exercise names is lower than 32.
- `dev check`: Check if exercise contains no tests and isn't marked with `test = false`.
### Changed
- The compilation time when installing Rustlings is reduced.
- Pressing `c` in the list for "continue on" now quits the list after setting the selected exercise as the current one.
- Better highlighting of the solution file after an exercise is done.
- Don't show the output of successful tests anymore. Instead, show the pretty output for tests.
- Be explicit about `q` only quitting the list and not the whole program in the list.
- Be explicit about `r` only resetting one exercise (the selected one) in the list.
- Ignore the standard output of `git init`.
- `threads3`: Remove the queue length and improve tests.
- `errors4`: Use match instead of a comparison chain in the solution.
- `functions3`: Only take `u8` to avoid using a too high number of iterations by mistake.
- `dev check`: Always check with strict Clippy (warnings to errors) when checking the solutions.
### Fixed
- Fix the error on some systems about too many open files during the final check of all exercises.
- Fix the list when the terminal height is too low.
- Restore the terminal after an error in the list.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the rust-lang/rustlings release v6.3.0, new exercise lints were added (forbid unsafe_code, unstable_features, todo, empty_loop; deny infinite_loop, mem_forget), a link to every exercise file is shown in the list, scroll padding and mouse scrolling were added, the help footer breaks into two lines on narrow terminals, dev check shows progress and checks exercise name length and test attribute, compilation time during installation was reduced, pressing c in the list quits after setting the current exercise, solution file highlighting was improved, successful test output is replaced with pretty output, q and r behaviors are clarified, git init stdout is ignored, threads3, errors4, and functions3 were improved, dev check uses strict Clippy, and fixes were made for too many open files, low terminal height, and terminal restoration after list errors.

chunks.title: Rustlings 6.4.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: rust-lang/rustlings
Release: Rustlings 6.4.0
Tag: v6.4.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2024-11-11T14:58:05Z; created_at: 2024-11-11T14:51:51Z
Release notes:
### Added
- The list of exercises is now searchable by pressing `s` or `/` 🔍️ (thanks to [@frroossst](https://github.com/frroossst))
- New option `c` in the prompt to manually check all exercises ✅ (thanks to [@Nahor](https://github.com/Nahor))
- New command `check-all` to manually check all exercises ✅ (thanks to [@Nahor](https://github.com/Nahor))
- Addictive animation for showing the progress of checking all exercises. A nice showcase of parallelism in Rust ✨
- New option `x` in the prompt to reset the file of the current exercise 🔄
- Allow `dead_code` for all exercises and solutions ⚰️ (thanks to [@huss4in](https://github.com/huss4in))
- Pause input while running an exercise to avoid unexpected prompt interactions ⏸️
- Limit the maximum number of exercises to 999. Any third-party exercises willing to reach that limit? 🔝
### Changed
- `enums3`: Remove redundant enum definition task (thanks to [@senekor](https://github.com/senekor))
- `if2`: Make the exercise less confusing by avoiding "fizz", "fuzz", "foo", "bar" and "baz" (thanks to [@senekor](https://github.com/senekor))
- `hashmap3`: Use the method `Entry::or_default`.
- Update the state of all exercises when checking all of them (thanks to [@Nahor](https://github.com/Nahor))
- The main prompt doesn't need a confirmation with ENTER on Unix-like systems anymore.
- No more jumping back to a previous exercise when its file is changed. Use the list to jump between exercises.
- Dump the solution file after an exercise is done even if the solution's directory doesn't exist.
- Rework the footer in the list.
- Optimize the file watcher.
### Fixed
- Fix bad contrast in the list on terminals with a light theme.
![out](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2bb3c7a7-f751-4972-9309-8566cf6e443c)
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the rust-lang/rustlings release v6.4.0, the exercise list is now searchable, new options c and x were added to the prompt, a new check-all command was added, an animation shows progress of checking all exercises, dead_code is allowed for all exercises and solutions, input is paused while running an exercise, the maximum number of exercises is limited to 999, enums3 and if2 were improved, hashmap3 uses Entry::or_default, the state of all exercises is updated when checking all, the main prompt no longer needs ENTER confirmation on Unix-like systems, jumping back to a previous exercise on file change was removed, the solution file is dumped even if the directory doesn't exist, the list footer was reworked, the file watcher was optimized, and bad contrast in the list on light terminals was fixed.

chunks.title: Rustlings 6.5.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: rust-lang/rustlings
Release: Rustlings 6.5.0
Tag: v6.5.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2025-08-21T22:07:22Z; created_at: 2025-08-21T22:05:34Z
Release notes:
### Added
- Check that Clippy is installed before initialization
### Changed
- Upgrade to Rust edition 2024
- Raise the minimum supported Rust version to `1.88`
- Don't follow symlinks in the file watcher
- `dev new`: Don't add `.rustlings-state.txt` to `.gitignore`
### Fixed
- Fix file links in VS Code
- Fix error printing when the progress bar is shown
- `dev check`: Don't check formatting if there are no solution files
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the rust-lang/rustlings release v6.5.0, a check that Clippy is installed before initialization was added, the Rust edition was upgraded to 2024, the minimum supported Rust version was raised to 1.88, symlinks are no longer followed in the file watcher, dev new no longer adds .rustlings-state.txt to .gitignore, file links in VS Code were fixed, error printing when the progress bar is shown was fixed, and dev check no longer checks formatting if there are no solution files.

questions.query_text: In the rust-lang/rustlings releases, which release adds a new intro section prefixing the normal exercises and also adds rust-analyzer support via an optional crate feature?

answer_support.answer_text: a new intro section prefixing the normal exercises has been added ... rust-analyzer support has been added via an optional crate feature

questions.query_text: Across the rust-lang/rustlings releases, how does the change to the exercise list in Rustlings 4.4.0 differ from the change to the exercise list in Rustlings v6.4.0?

options.text: 4.4.0 makes the list display structured data with filtering options and per-exercise progress, while v6.4.0 makes the list searchable and adds new prompt options c and x.
4.4.0 adds a search feature to the list, while v6.4.0 removes the list entirely.
Both releases only add a progress indicator to the list without other changes.
4.4.0 introduces the list command for the first time, while v6.4.0 adds mouse scrolling to the list.

questions.expected_answer: 4.4.0 makes the list display structured data with filtering options and per-exercise progress, while v6.4.0 makes the list searchable and adds new prompt options c and x.

answer_support.answer_text: rustlings list now displays structured data, and a takes a lot more options to filter that data ... also now shows you progress per-exercise
The list of exercises is now searchable ... New option c in the prompt to manually check all exercises ... New option x in the prompt to reset the file of the current exercise

questions.query_text: In the rust-lang/rustlings release window, order the following release-content states from earliest to latest: (1) a release that adds a progress indicator and a new rustlings lsp command, (2) a release that adds a new intro section and rust-analyzer support, (3) a release that makes the exercise list searchable and adds a check-all command, (4) a release that upgrades to Rust edition 2024 and raises the minimum supported Rust version to 1.88.

answer_support.answer_text: A new intro section prefixing the normal exercises has been added ... rust-analyzer support has been added
Adds a progress indicator, a new rustlings lsp command
The list of exercises is now searchable ... New command check-all
Upgrade to Rust edition 2024 ... Raise the minimum supported Rust version to 1.88
